L1.description: An authorization bypass exists in the gateway's WebSocket connection authentication flow where backend connections using shared-secret credentials can self-declare elevated authorization scopes that are not bound to any device identity. The server-side logic that should strip unbound client-declared scopes incorrectly exempts certain shared-authentication modes, allowing a remote attacker with basic shared credentials to retain admin-level scopes and perform privileged gateway operations.

ground_truth.reason: The WebSocket connect handler's scope-clearing logic exempted shared-token and shared-password authentication paths, allowing device-less shared-auth clients to self-declare elevated scopes such as operator.admin and perform admin-only gateway operations.
